substrate: marshmallow slug task (wt-with), same model (sonnet), 3-agent chain
knob: coordinate pack_max_tokens varied {4000, 2000, 1000}; channel = pack + handoff + scaffold actual tokens
note: Some first-batch trials hit a session rate-limit (429 turn-1 no-ops) and were discarded + re-run. All reported trials are valid real runs.
observations: Success HELD at 3/3 across the whole range — no failing 'too-terse' regime observed in [1000, 4000].
Channel shrank monotonically 8706 -> 6092 -> 4659 tokens as pack_max dropped.
Net USD DECREASED monotonically $1.123 -> $1.006 -> $0.855 (point estimates; N=3 cost variance is high and CIs overlap).
Turns rose at pack=1000 (51 vs 46) — the multi-turn tool tax the panel predicted (smaller pack -> more file reads) — but the pack input savings dominated, so net cost still fell.
verdict: No U-curve knee located within [1000, 4000]: success holds throughout and both channel and cost fall, so the default pack=4000 is OVER-PROVISIONED for this task. This is modestly BETTER than the pre-registered expectation (parity, never savings) — a small genuine USD reduction at held success. The too-terse upturn is below 1000 (untested); the firm finding is 'compress to 1000 with no success loss', the cost reduction is suggestive (overlapping CIs) not proven. Lever 1 remains defensive in spirit; here it also trimmed cost.
caveat: Single task; N=3 with high sonnet cost variance. A locked GO on a cost win needs N>=8 and a second task; the success-holds result is firm, the cost-win is directional.
